notes: This batch treats mix_control and mix_sweep as one Stage16 mixed-axis6 family.
run_stage16_sub4_axis6u.py and results_stage16_sub4_axis6u.json were read only as the exact uniform-baseline comparison anchor.
possible_downstream_consequence_policy: possible downstream consequence only; no direct A2-1 or A1 promotion from this batch

comparison_anchors.relevance: Catalogs mix_control, mix_sweep, and sub4_axis6u together under Stage16.
Top-level evidence pack was searched for the family SIM_IDs and no matches were found.
Absolute Stage16 baseline runner that matches control's uniform baseline exactly.
Absolute Stage16 baseline result surface with exact numeric identity to control U_* means.

runtime_expectations: run_stage16_axis6_mix_control.py writes one results_stage16_axis6_mix_control.json and one local evidence block for S_SIM_STAGE16_AXIS6_MIX_CONTROL.
run_stage16_axis6_mix_sweep.py writes one results_stage16_axis6_mix_sweep.json and one local evidence block for S_SIM_STAGE16_AXIS6_MIX_SWEEP.
Both mixed-axis6 runners use 512 random one-qubit states, the Se/Ne/Ni/Si terrain order, the same Stage16 outer/inner axis6 patterns, and the same operator parameter family.
failure_modes: The family can be mistaken for a fully evidenced Stage16 surface because the catalog lists all three related files, even though the top-level evidence pack contains none of their SIM_IDs.
sub4_axis6u can be mistakenly merged into the same bounded family because it numerically equals control's uniform baseline.
mix_control can be mistaken for a subset-equivalent of mix_sweep even though the two scripts use different comparison contracts and produce materially different effect sizes.

sims_split_rule.notes: This batch remains inside the separate sims source class and does not mix with ordinary theory/doc extraction.
